goal: Drain the 28 queued email attempts into provider_accepted by retrying SMTP sends, converting the credential-backlog into verified sends and pushing provider_accepted from 211 toward 239.
success_criteria: queued count reduced from 28 to near-zero
provider_accepted count advances by at least 20

slices.task: Drain the 28 queued emails. Step 1: Get all queued rows: sqlite3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/data/crm.sqlite "SELECT id,lead_id,recipient FROM email_attempts WHERE status='queued' ORDER BY id". Step 2: For each, retry SMTP send: GMAIL_ADDRESS='owner@outboundautonomy.com' python3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/scripts/gmail-smtp-send.py --to <email> --subject 'Website audit for your business' --body 'Hi, I run Outbound Autonomy. We scan business websites and show exactly what is costing you leads with a free audit and specific fixes at outboundautonomy.com/try.' Step 3: On success, update: sqlite3 ... "UPDATE email_attempts SET status='provider_accepted', provider_message_id='<msg_id>' WHERE id=<id>". On 535 auth failure, mark as failed and stop — credential is truly expired. Step 4: Write results to artifacts/missions/flush-queue-28-20260429-1620/bridge-results.md with: starting queued count, successful sends, failures, ending queued count, ending provider_accepted count.

reasoning: 28 emails queued — the credential issue is causing backlog. If SMTP still works (211 provider_accepted suggests it's intermittent), draining the queue could add 20+ verified sends in one burst. If the 535 error is permanent, this mission will detect it definitively and flag the credential as expired. Either outcome is valuable: sends or proof of credential failure. YELLOW because SMTP credentials may cause auth failures.
